notes: All 30 steps + 1 prerequisite completed. Created ffmpeg_render.py (RenderError, render_simple with NVENC, static crop, fade transitions), moviepy_render.py (ComplexRenderError, render_complex with per-frame dynamic crop via MoviePy), router.py (RoutingError, _classify_clip, route_and_render with EDL loading, dispatching, concat, audio mixing, subtitle generation). MoviePy import guarded with try/except for envs without it. 58 tests across 3 test files.

summary: Update autopilot/render/__init__.py to export public names from the three new modules; Write failing tests for ffmpeg_render.py public API surface: RenderError importable and is Exception, render_simple importable and callable with correct signature (edl_entry, crop_path, output_path, config), __all__ exports; Create ffmpeg_render.py with RenderError exception class, render_simple stub that raises NotImplementedError, and __all__ exports to pass API surface tests; Write failing tests for render_simple FFmpeg command construction: verify it calls subprocess.run with correct ffmpeg arguments including -c:v h264_nvenc, -crf, loudnorm audio filter with target LUFS from config, AAC encoding at config bitrate, -y overwrite flag, correct input/output paths; Implement render_simple: build FFmpeg filter graph with cuvid decode, optional static crop, scale_cuda to output resolution, NVENC H.264 High Profile encode, loudnorm audio to config LUFS, AAC at config bitrate. Call via subprocess.run(check=True). Return output path; Write failing tests for render_simple with static crop: when crop_path is a constant ndarray (same values all frames = static), verify the crop filter is included in ffmpeg command with correct x,y,w,h values; Implement static crop detection in render_simple: check if crop_path is not None and all rows are identical (static crop), if so add crop=x:y:w:h filter to FFmpeg filter graph before scale; Write failing tests for render_simple with no crop (crop_path is None): verify no crop filter in FFmpeg command. Also test render_simple error handling: subprocess.CalledProcessError is wrapped in RenderError; Implement no-crop path (skip crop filter when crop_path is None) and error handling: catch subprocess.CalledProcessError and raise RenderError with descriptive message; Write failing tests for render_simple xfade transitions: when edl_entry has a transition dict with type='crossfade' and duration, verify xfade filter is added to FFmpeg filter graph; Implement xfade transition support in render_simple: parse transition info from edl_entry, build xfade filter with specified duration and transition type; Write failing tests for moviepy_render.py public API surface: ComplexRenderError importable and is Exception, render_complex importable and callable with correct signature (edl_entry, crop_path, output_path, config), __all__ exports; Create moviepy_render.py with ComplexRenderError exception class, render_complex stub, and __all__ exports to pass API surface tests; Write failing tests for render_complex: verify it opens the source video, applies per-frame crop from crop_path array (shape N,2 with x,y offsets), scales to output resolution, and writes output file. Mock moviepy VideoFileClip and verify frame processing logic; Implement render_complex: use MoviePy 2.x VideoFileClip, subclip by in/out timecodes, apply per-frame crop using crop_path array, resize to output resolution, write output file with PyAV codec. Handle errors with ComplexRenderError; Write failing tests for render_complex edge cases: crop_path has fewer frames than video (should clamp to last), empty crop_path raises ComplexRenderError, output directory creation; Implement edge case handling in render_complex: clamp crop_path index to array length, validate crop_path is non-empty, create output directory if needed; Write failing tests for router.py public API surface: RoutingError importable and is Exception, route_and_render importable and callable with correct signature (narrative_id, db, config), __all__ exports; Create router.py with RoutingError exception class, route_and_render stub, and __all__ exports to pass API surface tests; Write failing tests for _classify_clip helper: clip with crop mode 'center' -> 'fast', 'manual_offset' -> 'fast', 'auto_subject' -> 'slow', clip with 'pip' overlay -> 'slow', clip with no crop_modes entry -> 'fast'; Implement _classify_clip: inspect EDL crop_modes and overlay settings to return 'fast' or 'slow' rendering path classification; Write failing tests for route_and_render EDL loading: when narrative has no edit plan, raises RoutingError. When edit plan exists, loads and parses edl_json correctly; Implement EDL loading in route_and_render: fetch edit plan from DB via db.get_edit_plan(), parse edl_json, raise RoutingError if missing; Write failing tests for route_and_render dispatching: verify fast-path clips call render_simple, slow-path clips call render_complex, each with correct arguments. Use mocked render functions; Implement clip dispatching in route_and_render: iterate EDL clips, classify each, compute crop paths as needed, call render_simple or render_complex accordingly, collect rendered segment paths; Write failing tests for final concatenation: after rendering all clips, verify route_and_render calls ffmpeg concat demuxer to join segments into final output, with correct audio mixing (source, music, voiceover tracks at EDL-specified levels); Implement final concatenation: build ffmpeg concat command to join rendered segments, mix audio tracks (source audio, music, voiceover) with amix/amerge filters at EDL-specified levels, output to output/{narrative_title}/final.mp4; Write failing tests for subtitle support in route_and_render: when config specifies subtitles and ASR transcript exists, verify drawtext/subtitles filter is applied in final output ffmpeg command; Implement subtitle support: load ASR transcript segments from DB, generate SRT file, apply subtitles filter in final concatenation ffmpeg command; Write failing tests for route_and_render error handling: render_simple failure on one clip raises RoutingError with context, missing narrative raises RoutingError, corrupt edl_json raises RoutingError; Implement comprehensive error handling in route_and_render: catch RenderError/ComplexRenderError from child renderers and wrap in RoutingError with clip context, validate narrative and EDL existence

Completed all 18 review fix steps (31-48). Fixes: (1) Removed unfailable try/except in test_loads_edl_from_edit_plan, (2) Added transform() behavioral assertions, (3) Replaced mkdtemp with TemporaryDirectory context manager, (4) Added clip.close() via try/finally in render_complex, (5) Implemented crop_path loading from DB via get_crop_path with list-to-dict crop_modes conversion, (6) Fixed transcript lookup to use media_id per-clip instead of narrative_id, (7) Fixed amix with [aout] label and -map stream routing, (8) Fixed -c copy vs filter conflict with 4-case conditional, (9) Merged subtitles into filter_complex when audio mixing active, (10) Added setpts=PTS-STARTPTS and replaced -to with -t duration. 73 tests passing.
